items.title: Classical linear and logistic regression
items.description: Small-to-medium datasets are often trained with full-batch GD or its closed-form alternatives; each epoch computes the exact gradient over all rows before updating coefficients.

items.title: Neural network pre-training on curated sets
items.description: When data fits in memory, researchers sometimes use large full-batch or near-full-batch steps for stable loss curves during early architecture experiments.

items.title: Fine-tuning embedding layers
items.description: Low-dimensional embedding tables on modest corpora can be optimized with batch GD when the parameter count and dataset size make per-epoch full passes affordable.

items.title: Teaching and debugging optimizers
items.description: Implementing batch GD from scratch on toy losses isolates the effect of η and update direction without mini-batch noise—standard in ML coursework and optimizer prototyping.
